The study/snug (7'10" x 7'7") offers a quiet workspace with a front aspect window, tiled floor, and radiator. Adjacent to the kitchen, the conservatory (10'11" x 10'7") features brick and double-glazed construction, with French doors opening onto the rear garden, underfloor heating, and tiled flooringâ€”ideal for relaxing or entertaining year-round. The dining room (11'1" x 9'9") also enjoys garden views with patio doors, tiled flooring, recessed downlights, and radiator. It provides access to a practical utility room (8'4" x 5'3") with integrated washing machine and tumble dryer, a rear access door, and a cupboard housing the boiler. First Floor The landing has a rear-facing window and loft access. The generous master bedroom (14'11" x 9'8") boasts two front-facing windows plus a charming arched feature window, twin built-in wardrobes, and radiator. A connecting dressing area features two further wardrobes, a built-in dresser, a side window, and access to a stylish en-suite shower room, complete with vanity basin, WC, shower cubicle, heated towel rail, extractor fan, and tiled walls and floor. There are three further well-proportioned bedrooms: Bedroom Two (12'4" x 8'2"): Double front aspect windows, built-in wardrobe, radiator Bedroom Three (12'4" x 6'10"): Rear window and radiator Bedroom Four (7'8" x 7'5"): Rear aspect, three built-in wardrobes, radiator The family bathroom includes an enclosed bath, pedestal basin, low-level WC, tiled walls and floor, radiator, shaver point, and side window. Outside The property benefits from both front and rear gardens, a driveway providing ample off-road parking, and a double garage. Additional features include gas central heating, double glazing throughout, and a bright, airy interior with thoughtful touches throughout. EPC Rating: D Council Tax Band: F

Summary: An attractive three bedroom home in sought-after Melton Park, offering light, well-planned living, a modern kitchen/diner, en-suite master, enclosed garden, summer house and garage. Set within 50 acres of parkland close to Woodbridge, golf, amenities and excellent transport links.

The light-filled and well-balanced accommodation is arranged over two floors. On the ground floor, the entrance hall leads to a cloakroom and a generous sitting room featuring an attractive fireplace. French doors provide direct access to the rear garden. Upstairs, the first floor landing includes an airing cupboard and leads to three bedrooms and the family bathroom. The master bedroom benefits from built-in wardrobes and an en-suite shower room. Outside The enclosed rear garden is mainly laid to lawn with a paved terrace ideal for outdoor dining, along with a summer house. A gate offers rear access to a pathway leading to the en bloc garage (17' × 8'4") with up-and-over door and pitched roof providing useful storage, as well as the nearby parking area.

Information on Air Source Heat Pumps- An air source heat pump works by absorbing heat from the environment, even when temperatures are well below freezing. It extracts heat from the air around us and transfers that heat to the inside of your home, keeping you warm and comfortable. Air Source Heat Pumps are more efficient, can be more cost effective and can reduce a properties carbon footprint. EPC- On Order Council Tax Band- B

Summary: Discover The Priory, an extraordinary Grade II Listed manor house offering 4,810 sq. ft (447 sq. m) of living space. Thought to date back to around 1586, this historic Suffolk property is situated in the idyllic Deben Valley, on the edge of a charming village.
Full description: Discover The Priory, an extraordinary Grade II Listed manor house offering 4,810 sq. ft (447 sq. m) of living space. Thought to date back to around 1586, this historic Suffolk property is situated in the idyllic Deben Valley, on the edge of a charming village. Architectural Grandeur and Modern Comfort Nestled in the Deben Valley, The Priory exemplifies Elizabethan architecture. This Grade II listed manor house, with origins around 1586, marries historic grandeur with contemporary comforts. Every detail, from intricate Tudor brick fireplaces to majestic octagonal chimneys and fine exposed chamfered timbers, tells a story of the past, while modern refurbishments ensure a comfortable lifestyle. Thoughtful Renovations Recent updates have revitalised The Priory, striking a balance between heritage charm and modern style. The property features new roofing, updated heating and plumbing systems, and modern wiring. Gleaming oak boarded floors and refurbished windows offer picturesque views of the surrounding landscape. Spacious and Inviting Interior Spanning 4,810 sq. ft (447 sq. m), The Priory welcomes you with high ceilings and sunlit rooms that create an airy, inviting atmosphere. The central reception/dining hall sets the scene for grand gatherings, while the drawing and sitting rooms provide intimate retreats. The highlight is the expansive family/living room with glazed elevations that blur the line between indoor and outdoor living. Here, panoramic views of the formal gardens enchant throughout the seasons. Enchanting Outdoor Spaces Outside, a landscape akin to a pastoral painting awaits. A five-bar gate opens to a winding driveway lined with Poplar trees. The formal gardens unfold like a tapestry, each "garden room" offering a new tableau of colour and fragrance. Explore well-kept lawns, wildflower meadows, hidden alcoves, and sun-dappled groves. A "secret" yew hedged pond garden invites quiet reflection, while a kitchen garden promises bountiful harvests. Picturesque lime trees and sun trap terraces provide perfect vantage points to enjoy the natural beauty. Prime Location and Accessibility Located in the heart of the Deben Valley, The Priory offers unparalleled tranquility while remaining close to modern amenities. The village of Brandeston, with its historic pub and esteemed prep school, provides a charming backdrop for daily life. Nearby towns Framlingham, Woodbridge, and Ipswich offer a wealth of shopping, dining, and cultural experiences. Excellent road and rail connections make London and beyond easily accessible, while the Suffolk Heritage Coast promises sandy shores and seaside serenity. A Unique Opportunity Whether seeking a peaceful retreat from city life or a historic masterpiece to call home, The Priory is ready to fulfil your desires. Lose yourself in the timeless beauty of this Elizabethan treasure, where history, elegance, and natural splendour harmoniously converge. Accommodation Highlights: - Three reception rooms - Kitchen and stunning open-plan living room - Six bedrooms - Four bathrooms - Outbuildings including granary barn, cart lodges, and workshop barns - Stable range - Formal gardens and meadow grounds running down to the River Deben
